directive.title: RFQ detail page with live data from /api/rfq/[id]

auditor.summary: The implementation correctly creates the API endpoint and server component page with proper error handling, headers, and data formatting. However, there are two HIGH-severity issues: (1) generateMetadata uses the database ID instead of the RFQ number for the page title, violating the spec requirement, and (2) the error handling in the page component may cause TypeScript strict mode issues and loses original error context. Additionally, there are MEDIUM concerns about import path validity and overly strict type guards. The test file is incomplete with placeholder tests. The build is recoverable with fixes, hence CONDITIONAL verdict.

top_issues.description: generateMetadata uses params.id instead of fetching the actual RFQ number from the database. The spec requires the title to contain the RFQ number (rfq.rfqNumber), not the database ID. This means the metadata title will show 'RFQ test-id-123 | FlowSeer' instead of 'RFQ RFQ-2024-001 | FlowSeer'.

The error catch clause uses 'unknown' type which may cause TypeScript strict mode issues. The error is logged but then a new Error is thrown with a generic message, losing the original error context. This violates EQS RELIABILITY requirements for proper error propagation.

The lineItems type guard function isValidLineItems() is overly strict and may reject valid data from the database. It requires all fields to be present and of specific types, but the schema might have optional fields or different types (e.g., unitPrice could be stored as string in JSON). This could cause valid line items to be silently dropped.
